params.name: Burghers of Calais
params.description:
On Jul 28, 1897 AD, the Burghers of Calais was unveiled in front of the City Hall in Calais, France.
During the Hundred Years' War between England and France, the French city of Calais was besieged by the English army for nearly two years. The citizens' lives were in danger, and after negotiations between both sides, King Edward III of England demanded that Calais choose six citizens to be executed, and these six people had to leave the city with shaved heads, barefoot, and with locked necks, …
